MAR.topRegainer: Yassine BOUNOU / Chadi RIAD / Neil EL AYNAOUI

BRA.name: VINICIUS JUNIOR
BRA.highlights: Match-equalising goal min 31 (Right Foot, Ball Progression). 11 ball progressions, 10 take-ons — most in Brazil. 34.1 km/h top speed, 60 sprints. Constant threat down the left with dynamic one-vs-one ability.

BRA.name: RAPHINHA
BRA.highlights: Primary cross deliverer: 6 cross attempts, 3 completed. 2 shots on target. 11,651m distance covered — team high. 152 high-speed runs, 80 sprints. Led Brazil's wide right attacking output for the full 90+ minutes.

BRA.name: DOUGLAS SANTOS
BRA.highlights: Brazil's top direct presser: 14 direct pressures. 8 possession regains (joint-team high). 20 line break attempts, 17 completed (85%). Relentless left-back combining defensive solidity with forward drive.

BRA.name: MARQUINHOS
BRA.highlights: 8 possession regains (joint-team high). 3 aerial duels contested. Blocked 3 shots. Commanding presence at centre-back, orchestrating Brazil's defensive shape across 90+ minutes.

MAR.name: Ismael SAIBARI
MAR.highlights: Scored Morocco's opening goal min 20 (Right Foot, Pass). 3 shots on target — most in Morocco. 76 sprints — team high. 63 indirect pressures in relentless high-energy press. Decisive in Morocco's decisive attacking moments.

MAR.name: Neil EL AYNAOUI
MAR.highlights: Morocco's top direct presser: 8 direct pressures. 7 possession regains (joint-top). 11,778m total distance — second-highest in team. Box-to-box engine combining defensive solidity with 3 shots attempted.

MAR.name: Ayyoub BOUADDI
MAR.highlights: Morocco's distance leader: 11,871m covered. 69 offers to receive — team high. 66 indirect pressures. 15 line breaks completed from 15 attempts (100%). Midfield anchor dictating Morocco's rhythm.

MAR.name: Achraf HAKIMI
MAR.highlights: 2 completed crosses from 4 attempts. 5 ball progressions, 3 step-ins. 3 shots attempted. 10,149m distance. Dynamic right-back central to Morocco's wide attacking play and transition speed.

BRA.summary: Brazil dominated possession at 47% and pressed aggressively with 315 total pressures and 54 direct pressures, but conceded early to Morocco's swift counter-attack at minute 20. After equalising through VINICIUS JUNIOR (min 31), they generated 5 shots on target but failed to break Morocco's compact defensive mid-block, which occupied 34% of the opposition's out-of-possession time.
strengths: High-intensity pressing structure: 315 total pressures, 54 direct, 133 pushing-on-into-pressing sequences creating sustained forward disruption
Attacking quality through VINICIUS JUNIOR's ball progressions (11) and take-ons (10 attempts), and RAPHINHA's cross delivery (6 open-play attempts)
Wide overloads with DOUGLAS SANTOS and VINICIUS JUNIOR combining down the left, generating Morocco's most defensive pressure zones
weaknesses: High defensive line exposed to Morocco's quick transitions — conceded from a sharp counter-attack at min 20 before Brazil could reorganise
Only 5 shots on target from 12 attempts despite territorial dominance in the second half
Cross delivery inconsistency: 13 attempted, only 5 completed in open play (38% conversion)

MAR.summary: Morocco executed a disciplined defensive and counter-attacking gameplan, scoring through a swift transition (min 20) and holding the lead before Brazil equalised at min 31. Their compact mid-block (34% of out-of-possession sequences) frustrated Brazil's build-up, with SAIBARI and EL AYNAOUI providing the attacking and defensive engine from midfield.
strengths: Swift counter-attacking transitions through SAIBARI, BRAHIM DIAZ and EL KHANNOUSS — exploiting space behind Brazil's high defensive line
High defensive energy: 285 pressures, 50 forced turnovers, 47 possession regains, with EL AYNAOUI and BOUADDI leading recovery work
Disciplined mid-block shape: 34% of out-of-possession time in organised mid-block denied Brazil central penetration
weaknesses: Unable to protect the lead after scoring — conceded the equaliser just 11 minutes after taking the lead, suggesting fragility in defending set-piece situations
Low cross completion rate: only 2 from 9 open-play cross attempts (22%), limiting wide-area delivery effectiveness
GK BOUNOU's low pass completion (54%, 39 att, 21 comp) under Brazil's pressing forced longer distribution that ceded possession regularly
